When assertMode is true\n69\t// and validation fails, it returns a detailed error message showing expected\n70\t// vs actual values along with response context.\n71\t//\n72\t// Parameters:\n73\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n74\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n75\t//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure; if false, return bool only\n76\t//\n77\t// Returns:\n78\t//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information\n79\t//\n80\t// Example (boolean mode):\n81\t//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 200, false)\n82\t//   if !result.Match {\n83\t//       // Handle non-200 case\n84\t//   }\n85\t//\n86\t// Example (assertion mode):\n87\t//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 404, true)\n88\t//   if !result.Match {\n89\t//       t.Error(result.Error)  // Full error with expected vs actual\n90\t//   }\n91\tfunc AssertStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n92\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n93\t\n94\t\t// Build response context for error messages\n95\t\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n96\t\n97\t\t// Check if status codes match\n98\t\tmatches := actualCode == expectedCode\n99\t\n100\t\tif matches {\n101\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n102\t\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n103\t\t\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n104\t\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n105\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: \"\",\n109\t\t\t}\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\t// Build detailed error message\n113\t\terrorMsg := GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n125\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n126\t\t\tError:              errorMsg,\n127\t\t}\n128\t}\n129\t\n130\t// AssertStatusCodeAny validates status errorMsg,\n197\t\t}\n198\t}\n199\t\n200\t// buildStatusCodeMismatchError creates a detailed error message for status code mismatches.\n201\t//\n202\t// This helper builds a comprehensive error message that includes:\n203\t// - The expected status code with description\n204\t// - The actual status code with description\n205\t// - Response object context (type and status if available)\n206\tfunc GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n212\t\n213\t\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n214\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n215\t\t}\n216\t\n217\t\treturn b.String()\n218\t}\n219\t\n220\t// buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny creates a detailed error message for multi-code mismatches.\n221\t//\n222\t// This helper builds a comprehensive error message when {\n232\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n233\t\t}\n234\t\n235\t\treturn b.String()\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t// getStatusCodeResponseContext extracts contextual information about the response object.\n239\t//\n240\t// This helper builds a string description of the response object for error messages.\n241\t// For status code validation, the context includes the response type.\n242\tfunc getStatusCodeResponseContext(response interface{}) string {\n243\t\tswitch response.(type) {\n244\t\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n245\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"httptest.ResponseRecorder\")\n246\t\tcase *http.Response:\n247\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"http.Response\")\n248\t\tdefault:\n249\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown type: %T\", response)\n250\t\t}\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t// formatCodeList formats a list of status codes with descriptions for error messages.\n254\t//\n255\t// This helper creates a readable string representation of multiple allowed codes,\n256\t// including their descriptions for better debugging.\n257\tfunc formatCodeList(codes []int) string {\n258\t\tvar parts []string\n259\t\tfor _, code := range codes {\n260\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, GetStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n261\t\t}\n262\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n263\t}\n264\t\n265\t// ValidateStatusCode validates that the HTTP response has the expected status code.\n266\t//\n267\t// This is the basic status code validation helper that checks if a response\n268\t// matches a single expected status code. It provides clear error messages\n269\t// indicating what was expected vs. what was received.\n270\t//\n271\t// Parameters:\n272\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n273\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n274\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n275\t//\n276\t// Example:\n277\t//   ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n278\tfunc ValidateStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}, expectedCode int) {\n279\t\tt.Helper()\n280\t\n281\t\tresult := AssertStatusCode(response, expectedCode, true)\n282\t\n283\t\tif !result.Match {\n284\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: %d (%s)\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n285\t\t\t\tresult.Expected, result.ExpectedDescription,\n286\t\t\t\tresult.Actual, result.ActualDescription,\n287\t\t\t\tresult.ResponseContext)\n288\t\t}\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t// ValidateStatusCodeAny validates that the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n292\t//\n293\t// This helper is useful when multiple status codes are acceptable for a given scenario.\n294\t// For example, when testing authentication, you might accept both 403 (Forbidden)\n295\t// and 401 (Unauthorized) depending on the authentication method.\n296\t//\n297\t// Parameters:\n298\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n299\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n300\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n301\t//\n302\t// Example:\n303\t//   ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204})\n304\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAny(t *testing.T, response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) {\n305\t\tt.Helper()\n306\t\n307\t\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n308\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"ValidateStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\")\n309\t\t\treturn\n310\t\t}\n311\t\n312\t\tresult := AssertStatusCodeAny(response, allowedCodes, true)\n313\t\n314\t\tif !result.Match {\n315\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: one of [%s]\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n316\t\t\t\tresult.ExpectedDescription, result.Actual, result.ActualDescription, result.ResponseContext)\n317\t\t}\n318\t}\n319\t\n320\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange validates that the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n321\t//\n322\t// This helper is useful for validating status codes by category, such as:\n323\t// - 2xx for success responses (200-299)\n324\t// - 4xx for client errors (400-499)\n325\t// - 5xx for server errors (500-599)\n326\t//\n327\t// Parameters:\n328\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n329\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n330\t//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n331\t//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n332\t//\n333\t// Example:\n334\t//   // Validate 2xx success response\n335\t//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299)\n336\t//\n337\t//   // Validate 4xx client error\n338\t//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 400, 499)\n339\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(t *testing.T, response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) {\n340\t\tt.Helper()\n341\t\n342\t\tif minCode > maxCode {\n343\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"ValidateStatusCodeRange: minCode (%d) cannot be greater than maxCode (%d)\", minCode, maxCode)\n344\t\t}\n345\t\n346\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n347\t\n348\t\tif actualCode < minCode || actualCode > maxCode {\n349\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code in range [%d-%d] (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n350\t\t\t\tminCode, maxCode, getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode),\n351\t\t\t\tactualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n352\t\t}\n353\t}\n354\t\n355\t// CheckStatusCode checks if the HTTP response has the expected status code without asserting.\n356\t//\n357\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean, allowing for conditional logic\n358\t// in tests. Useful when you need to handle different status codes programmatically\n359\t// rather than failing the test immediately.\n360\t//\n361\t// Parameters:\n362\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n363\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n364\t//\n365\t// Returns:\n366\t//   - true if the response has the expected status code, false otherwise\n367\t//\n368\t// Example:\n369\t//   if CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n370\t//       // Handle 404 case\n371\t//   } else {\n372\t//       // Handle other cases\n373\t//   }\n374\tfunc CheckStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int) bool {\n375\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n376\t\treturn actualCode == expectedCode\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\t// CheckStatusCodeAny checks if the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n380\t//\n381\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for flexible status code checking.\n382\t// Useful when you need to handle multiple acceptable status codes programmatically.\n383\t//\n384\t// Parameters:\n385\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n386\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n387\t//\n388\t// Returns:\n389\t//   - true if the response status code is in the allowed list, false otherwise\n390\t//\n391\t// Example:\n392\t//   allowedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n393\t//   if CheckStatusCodeAny(w, allowedCodes) {\n394\t//       // Handle success case\n395\t//   } else {\n396\t//       // Handle error case\n397\t//   }\n398\tfunc CheckStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) bool {\n399\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n400\t\n401\t\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n402\t\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n403\t\t\t\treturn true\n404\t\t\t}\n405\t\t}\n406\t\treturn false\n407\t}\n408\t\n409\t// CheckStatusCodeRange checks if the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n410\t//\n411\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for range-based status code validation.\n412\t// Useful for category-based status code checking (e.g., 2xx, 4xx, 5xx).\n413\t//\n414\t// Parameters:\n415\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n416\t//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n417\t//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n418\t//\n419\t// Returns:\n420\t//   - true if the status code is within the range, false otherwise\n421\t//\n422\t// Example:\n423\t//   // Check for any success code (2xx)\n424\t//   if CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n425\t//       // Handle success\n426\t//   }\n427\tfunc CheckStatusCodeRange(response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) bool {\n428\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n429\t\treturn actualCode >= minCode && actualCode <= maxCode\n430\t}\n431\t\n432\t// GetStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for an HTTP status code.\n433\t//\n434\t// This helper provides descriptive names for common HTTP status codes, making\n435\t// test error messages more informative and easier to understand.\n436\t//\n437\t// Parameters:\n438\t//   - code: The HTTP status code\n439\t//\n440\t// Returns:\n441\t//   - A human-readable description string (e.g., \"Not Found\" for 404)\n442\t//\n443\t// Example:\n444\t//   desc := GetStatusCodeDescription(404)\n445\t//   // desc = \"Not Found\"\n446\tfunc GetStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n447\t\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n448\t\t\t100: \"Continue\",\n449\t\t\t101: \"Switching Protocols\",\n450\t\t\t102: \"Processing\",\n451\t\t\t200: \"OK\",\n452\t\t\t201: \"Created\",\n453\t\t\t202: \"Accepted\",\n454\t\t\t203: \"Non-Authoritative Information\",\n455\t\t\t204: \"No Content\",\n456\t\t\t205: \"Reset Content\",\n457\t\t\t206: \"Partial Content\",\n458\t\t\t300: \"Multiple Choices\",\n459\t\t\t301: \"Moved Permanently\",\n460\t\t\t302: \"Found\",\n461\t\t\t303: \"See Other\",\n462\t\t\t304: \"Not Modified\",\n463\t\t\t305: \"Use Proxy\",\n464\t\t\t307: \"Temporary Redirect\",\n465\t\t\t308: \"Permanent Redirect\",\n466\t\t\t400: \"Bad Request\",\n467\t\t\t401: \"Unauthorized\",\n468\t\t\t402: \"Payment Required\",\n469\t\t\t403: \"Forbidden\",\n470\t\t\t404: \"Not Found\",\n471\t\t\t405: \"Method Not Allowed\",\n472\t\t\t406: \"Not Acceptable\",\n473\t\t\t407: \"Proxy Authentication Required\",\n474\t\t\t408: \"Request Timeout\",\n475\t\t\t409: \"Conflict\",\n476\t\t\t410: \"Gone\",\n477\t\t\t411: \"Length Required\",\n478\t\t\t412: \"Precondition Failed\",\n479\t\t\t413: \"Payload Too Large\",\n480\t\t\t414: \"URI Too Long\",\n481\t\t\t415: \"Unsupported Media Type\",\n482\t\t\t416: \"Range Not Satisfiable\",\n483\t\t\t417: \"Expectation Failed\",\n484\t\t\t418: \"I'm Required\",\n490\t\t\t429: \"Too Many Requests\",\n491\t\t\t431: \"Request Header Fields Too Large\",\n492\t\t\t451: \"Unavailable For Legal Reasons\",\n493\t\t\t500: \"Internal Server Supported\",\n499\t\t\t506: \"Variant Also Negotiates\",\n500\t\t\t507: \"Insufficient Storage\",\n501\t\t\t508: \"Loop Detected\",\n502\t\t\t510: \"Not Extended\",\n503\t\t\t511: \"Network Authentication Required\",\n504\t\t}\n505\t\n506\t\tif desc, ok := descriptions[code]; ok {\n507\t\t\treturn desc\n508\t\t}\n509\t\treturn \"Unknown\"\n510\t}\n511\t\n512\t// getRangeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n513\t//\n514\t// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges,\n515\t// such as \"Success\" for 2xx codes or \"Client Error\" for 4xx codes.\n516\tfunc getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n517\t\tranges := map[string]string{\n518\t\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n519\t\t\t\"300-399\": server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"testing\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HTTP STATUS CODE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// This module provides comprehensive HTTP status code validation utilities\n// for error response testing. It supports single status codes, multiple allowed\n// status codes, and flexible validation patterns.\n//\n// Functions:\n// - ValidateStatusCode: Validate single expected status code\n// - ValidateStatusCodeAny: Validate against multiple allowed status codes\n// - ValidateStatusCodeRange: Validate status code is within range\n// - CheckStatusCode: Non-asserting version that returns boolean\n// - AssertStatusCode: Flexible validation supporting both assertion and boolean modes\n// - AssertStatusCodeAny: Flexible multi-code validation with assertion mode\n// - StatusCodeMatchResult: Detailed validation result with error context\n// - GetStatusCodeDescription: Get human-readable status code description\n// =============================================================================\n\n// StatusCodeMatchResult contains detailed information about a status code validation.\n//\n// This type provides comprehensive error context for debugging validation failures,\n// including the expected and actual status codes with their descriptions, the\n// response object context, and a formatted error message.\ntype StatusCodeMatchResult struct {\n\t// Match indicates whether the status code matched successfully\n\tMatch bool\n\n\t// Expected is the status code that was expected\n\tExpected int\n\n\t// Actual is the status code that was received\n\tActual int\n\n\t// ExpectedDescription is the human-readable description of the expected status code\n\tExpectedDescription string\n\n\t// ActualDescription is the human-readable description of the actual status code\n\tActualDescription string\n\n\t// ResponseContext contains information about the response object\n\tResponseContext string\n\n\t// Error is a formatted error message describing the mismatch\n\tError string\n}\n\n// String returns a human-readable representation of the validation result.\nfunc (r StatusCodeMatchResult) String() string {\n\tif r.Match {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code match: %d (%s)\", r.Actual, r.ActualDescription)\n\t}\n\treturn r.Error\n}\n\n// AssertStatusCode validates status code with flexible assertion mode.\n//\n// This function supports both boolean-only mode (for conditional logic) and\n// assertion mode (with descriptive error messages). When assertMode is true\n// and validation fails, it returns a detailed error message showing expected\n// vs actual values along with response context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure; if false, return bool only\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information\n//\n// Example (boolean mode):\n//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 200, false)\n//   if !result.Match {\n//       // Handle non-200 case\n//   }\n//\n// Example (assertion mode):\n//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 404, true)\n//   if !result.Match {\n//       t.Error(result.Error)  // Full error with expected vs actual\n//   }\nfunc AssertStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\t// Build response context for error messages\n\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n\n\t// Check if status codes match\n\tmatches := actualCode == expectedCode\n\n\tif matches {\n\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n\t\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build detailed error message\n\terrorMsg := buildStatusCodeMismatchError(\n\t\texpectedCode,\n\t\tactualCode,\n\t\tresponseContext,\n\t)\n\n\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\tMatch:              false,\n\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\tError:              errorMsg,\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertStatusCodeAny validates status code against multiple allowed codes.\n//\n// This function is similar to AssertStatusCode but accepts multiple allowed\n// status codes. Returns detailed error information when validation fails,\n// including the full list of allowed status codes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable status codes\n//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information\n//\n// Example:\n//   result := AssertStatusCodeAny(w, []int{200, 201, 204}, true)\n//   if !result.Match {\n//       t.Error(result.Error)\n//   }\nfunc AssertStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\t\tMatch:              false,\n\t\t\tExpected:           0,\n\t\t\tActual:             0,\n\t\t\tExpectedDescription: \"\",\n\t\t\tActualDescription:   \"\",\n\t\t\tResponseContext:     getStatusCodeResponseContext(response),\n\t\t\tError:              \"AssertStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n\n\t// Check if actual code matches any in the allowed list\n\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n\t\t\tallowedDesc := formatCodeList(allowedCodes)\n\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n\t\t\t\tExpected:           actualCode,\n\t\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: allowedDesc,\n\t\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build helpful error message showing what was allowed vs. what was received\n\tallowedDesc := formatCodeList(allowedCodes)\n\terrorMsg := buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(\n\t\tallowedDesc,\n\t\tactualCode,\n\t\tresponseContext,\n\t)\n\n\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\tMatch:              false,\n\t\tExpected:           0,\n\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\tExpectedDescription: allowedDesc,\n\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\tError:              errorMsg,\n\t}\n}\n\n// buildStatusCodeMismatchError creates a detailed error message for status code mismatches.\n//\n// This helper builds a comprehensive error message that includes:\n// - The expected status code with description\n// - The actual status code with description\n// - Response object context (type and status if available)\nfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchError(expected, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n\tvar b strings.Builder\n\n\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", expected, GetStatusCodeDescription(expected)))\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n\n\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n\t}\n\n\treturn b.String()\n}\n\n// buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny creates a detailed error message for multi-code mismatches.\n//\n// This helper builds a comprehensive error message when validating against multiple\n// allowed status codes, showing all allowed codes vs the actual received code.\nfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(allowedList string, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n\tvar b strings.Builder\n\n\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: one of [%s]\\n\", allowedList))\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n\n\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n\t}\n\n\treturn b.String()\n}\n\n// getStatusCodeResponseContext extracts contextual information about the response object.\n//\n// This helper builds a string description of the response object for error messages.\n// For status code validation, the context includes the response type.\nfunc getStatusCodeResponseContext(response interface{}) string {\n\tswitch response.(type) {\n\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"httptest.ResponseRecorder\")\n\tcase *http.Response:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"http.Response\")\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown type: %T\", response)\n\t}\n}\n\n// formatCodeList formats a list of status codes with descriptions for error messages.\n//\n// This helper creates a readable string representation of multiple allowed codes,\n// including their descriptions for better debugging.\nfunc formatCodeList(codes []int) string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor _, code := range codes {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, GetStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n\t}\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCode validates that the HTTP response has the expected status code.\n//\n// This is the basic status code validation helper that checks if a response\n// matches a single expected status code. It provides clear error messages\n// indicating what was expected vs. what was received.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\nfunc ValidateStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}, expectedCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tresult := AssertStatusCode(response, expectedCode, true)\n\n\tif !result.Match {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: %d (%s)\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n\t\t\tresult.Expected, result.ExpectedDescription,\n\t\t\tresult.Actual, result.ActualDescription,\n\t\t\tresult.ResponseContext)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeAny validates that the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n//\n// This helper is useful when multiple status codes are acceptable for a given scenario.\n// For example, when testing authentication, you might accept both 403 (Forbidden)\n// and 401 (Unauthorized) depending on the authentication method.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204})\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeAny(t *testing.T, response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"ValidateStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tresult := AssertStatusCodeAny(response, allowedCodes, true)\n\n\tif !result.Match {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: one of [%s]\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n\t\t\tresult.ExpectedDescription, result.Actual, result.ActualDescription, result.ResponseContext)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeRange validates that the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n//\n// This helper is useful for validating status codes by category, such as:\n// - 2xx for success responses (200-299)\n// - 4xx for client errors (400-499)\n// - 5xx for server errors (500-599)\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//\n// Example:\n//   // Validate 2xx success response\n//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299)\n//\n//   // Validate 4xx client error\n//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 400, 499)\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(t *testing.T, response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif minCode > maxCode {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"ValidateStatusCodeRange: minCode (%d) cannot be greater than maxCode (%d)\", minCode, maxCode)\n\t}\n\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\tif actualCode < minCode || actualCode > maxCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code in range [%d-%d] (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n\t\t\tminCode, maxCode, getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode),\n\t\t\tactualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n\t}\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCode checks if the HTTP response has the expected status code without asserting.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean, allowing for conditional logic\n// in tests. Useful when you need to handle different status codes programmatically\n// rather than failing the test immediately.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the response has the expected status code, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   if CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n//       // Handle 404 case\n//   } else {\n//       // Handle other cases\n//   }\nfunc CheckStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int) bool {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\treturn actualCode == expectedCode\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCodeAny checks if the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for flexible status code checking.\n// Useful when you need to handle multiple acceptable status codes programmatically.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the response status code is in the allowed list, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   allowedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n//   if CheckStatusCodeAny(w, allowedCodes) {\n//       // Handle success case\n//   } else {\n//       // Handle error case\n//   }\nfunc CheckStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) bool {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCodeRange checks if the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for range-based status code validation.\n// Useful for category-based status code checking (e.g., 2xx, 4xx, 5xx).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the status code is within the range, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   // Check for any success code (2xx)\n//   if CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n//       // Handle success\n//   }\nfunc CheckStatusCodeRange(response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) bool {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\treturn actualCode >= minCode && actualCode <= maxCode\n}\n\n// GetStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for an HTTP status code.\n//\n// This helper provides descriptive names for common HTTP status codes, making\n// test error messages more informative and easier to understand.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - code: The HTTP status code\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - A human-readable description string (e.g., \"Not Found\" for 404)\n//\n// Example:\n//   desc := GetStatusCodeDescription(404)\n//   // desc = \"Not Found\"\nfunc GetStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n\t\t100: \"Continue\",\n\t\t101: \"Switching Protocols\",\n\t\t102: \"Processing\",\n\t\t200: \"OK\",\n\t\t201: \"Created\",\n\t\t202: \"Accepted\",\n\t\t203: \"Non-Authoritative Information\",\n\t\t204: \"No Content\",\n\t\t205: \"Reset Content\",\n\t\t206: \"Partial Content\",\n\t\t300: \"Multiple Choices\",\n\t\t301: \"Moved Permanently\",\n\t\t302: \"Found\",\n\t\t303: \"See Other\",\n\t\t304: \"Not Modified\",\n\t\t305: \"Use Proxy\",\n\t\t307: \"Temporary Redirect\",\n\t\t308: \"Permanent Redirect\",\n\t\t400: \"Bad Request\",\n\t\t401: \"Unauthorized\",\n\t\t402: \"Payment Required\",\n\t\t403: \"Forbidden\",\n\t\t404: \"Not Found\",\n\t\t405: \"Method Not Allowed\",\n\t\t406: \"Not Acceptable\",\n\t\t407: \"Proxy Authentication Required\",\n\t\t408: \"Request Timeout\",\n\t\t409: \"Conflict\",\n\t\t410: \"Gone\",\n\t\t411: \"Length Required\",\n\t\t412: \"Precondition Failed\",\n\t\t413: \"Payload Too Large\",\n\t\t414: \"URI Too Long\",\n\t\t415: \"Unsupported Media Type\",\n\t\t416: \"Range Not Satisfiable\",\n\t\t417: \"Expectation Failed\",\n\t\t418: \"I'm a teapot\",\n\t\t422: \"Unprocessable Entity\",\n\t\t423: \"Locked\",\n\t\t424: \"Failed Dependency\",\n\t\t426: \"Upgrade Required\",\n\t\t428: \"Precondition Required\",\n\t\t429: \"Too Many Gateway\",\n\t\t503: \"Service Unavailable\",\n\t\t504: \"Gateway Timeout\",\n\t\t505: \"HTTP Version Not Supported\",\n\t\t506: \"Variant Also Negotiates\",\n\t\t507: \"Insufficient Storage\",\n\t\t508: \"Loop Detected\",\n\t\t510: \"Not Extended\",\n\t\t511: \"Network Authentication Required\",\n\t}\n\n\tif desc, ok := descriptions[code]; ok {\n\t\treturn desc\n\t}\n\treturn \"Unknown\"\n}\n\n// getRangeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n//\n// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges,\n// such as \"Success\" for 2xx codes or \"Client Error\" for 4xx codes.\nfunc getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n\tranges := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n\t\t\"300-399\": \"Redirection\",\n\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n\t}\n\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n\t\treturn desc\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Codes %d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n}\n\n// getStatusCode extracts the HTTP status code from various response types.\n//\n// This helper supports both *httptest.ResponseRecorder and *http.Response,\n// making the validation functions work with different response types.\nfunc getStatusCode(response interface{}) int {\n\tswitch r := response.(type) {\n\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n\t\treturn r.Code\n\tcase *http.Response:\n\t\treturn r.StatusCode\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"Unsupported response type: %T. {\n749\t\tif fixture.StatusCode < 100 || fixture.StatusCode >= 600 {\n750\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code: %d\", fixture.StatusCode)\n751\t\t}\n752\t\n753\t\tif fixture.ErrorCode == \"\" ERROR RESPONSE FIXTURES\n// =============================================================================\n// This file provides reusable test fixtures for common HTTP error scenarios.\n// These fixtures are designed to be easily configurable and loadable for different\n// endpoints and error contexts, complementing the existing test infrastructure.\n//\n// Fixtures vs Error Patterns:\n// - Error Patterns (error_test_patterns.go): Define expected behaviors for validation\n// - HTTP Fixtures (this file): Create mock responses for testing\n// Use fixtures when you need to simulate server responses, use patterns when validating them.\n//\n// When to use HTTP fixtures:\n// - Mocking server error responses in integration tests\n// - Creating expected responses for validation tests\n// - Testing client error handling behavior\n// - Building response samples for documentation\n// - Writing tests that need complete HTTP response objects\n//\n// When to use Error Patterns instead:\n// - Defining expected test outcomes\n// - Validating actual server responses\n// - Table-driven test scenarios\n// - Reusable validation expectations\n//\n// Available fixtures:\n// - 404 Not Found (with customizable path/message)\n// - 405 Method Not Allowed (with customizable allowed methods)\n// - 415 Unsupported Media Type\n// - 500 Internal Server Error (generic)\n// - 400 Bad Request\n// - 401 Unauthorized\n// - 403 Forbidden\n// - 409 Conflict\n//\n// Usage:\n//   // Create a fixture for a missing resource\n//   fixture := NotFoundFixture(\"/api/blobs/missing-file.txt\", \"\", \"\")\n// assert.Equal(t, pattern.ExpectedStatus, response.StatusCode)\n//\n// Bead: bf-7d2vgf\n// Created: 2026-07-14\n// =============================================================================\n\n// HTTPErrorFixture represents a configurable HTTP error response fixture.\n//\n// Provides a complete error response structure with HTTP status, content type,\n// and error body that can be customized field must be present\n\tRequireMessage bool\n\n\t// ExpectedCode is the specific error code value expected (empty = any code is valid)\n\tExpectedCode string\n\n\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum S3Error struct {\n\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Error\"`\n\tCode    string   `xml:\"Code\"`\n\tMessage string   `xml:\"Message\"`\n\n\t// Optional fields that may be present in error responses\n\tResource  string `xml:\"Resource,omitempty\"`\n\tRequestId string `xml:\"RequestId,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents a single validation error with context.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\t// Field is the name of the field that failed validation (empty for structural errors)\n\tField string\n\n\t// Message is a human-readable description of the validation failure\n\tMessage string\n\n\t// Path is the dot-notation path to the invalid field (for nested structures)\n\tPath string\n}\n\n// ValidationResult contains the results of error response validation.\ntype ValidationResult struct {\n\t// Valid indicates whether the validation passed\n\tValid bool\n\n\t// Errors is a list of validation errors (empty if Valid is true)\n\tErrors []ValidationError\n\n\t// ParsedError is the ErrorStructureValidationOptions {\n\treturn ErrorStructureValidationOptions{\n\t\tRequireCode:      true,\n\t\tRequireMessage:   true,\n\t\tExpectedCode:     \"\",\n\t\tMinMessageLength:  10,\n\t\tMessageContains:  \"\",\n\t\tCustomFields:     make(map[string]string),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple validates error response structure with default options.\n//\n// characters)\n// - Non-empty Message field (at least 10 characters)\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - body: The raw response body bytes\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tresult := ValidateErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(body, options)\n\n\t// If validation failed, report errors to test\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfor _, err := range result.Errors {\n\t\t\tif err.Field != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Field '%s' validation failed: %s\", err.Field, err.Message)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Validation failed: %s\", err.Message)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result.Valid\n}\n\n// AssertValidErrorResponseStructure asserts that the error response structure is valid.\n//\n// This is a convenience function for basic validation assertions.\n// It will fail the test if validation fails.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - body: The raw response body bytes\n//\n// Example:\n//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody)\nfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body }\n//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t, body, opts)\nfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tresult := ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, options)\n\tif !result {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed with options\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CORE VALIDATION LOGIC\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructureWithOptions performs the actual validation logic.\n//\n// This helper function contains the core validation logic used by all public\n// validation functions. It returns a detailed ValidationResult that can be\n// used for reporting or assertions.\nfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) err),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\tresult.ParsedError = &errorResponse\n\n\t// Validate required Code field\n\tif options.RequireCode {\n\t\tif strings.TrimSpace(errorResponse.Code) == \"\" {\n\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tField:   \"Code\",\n\t\t\t\tPath:    \"Error.Code\",\n\t\t\t\tMessage: \"Code field is required but is empty or missing\",\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate required Message field\n\tif options.RequireMessage {\n\t\ttrimmedMessage := strings.TrimSpace(errorResponse.Message)\n\t\tif trimmedMessage == \"\" {\n\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tField:   \"Message\",\n\t\t\t\tPath:    \"Error.Message\",\n\t\t\t\tMessage: \"Message field is required but is empty or missing\",\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Check minimum length (only if message exists)\n\t\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 && len(trimmedMessage) < options.MinMessageLength {\n\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\t\tField:   \"Message\",\n\t\t\t\t\tPath:    \"Error.Message\",\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\"Message field is too short: %d characters (minimum: %d)\", len(trimmedMessage), options.MinMessageLength),\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate expected Code value (if specified)\n\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && errorResponse.Code != \"\" {\n\t\tif errorResponse.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tField:   \"Code\",\n\t\t\t\tPath:    \"Error.Code\",\n\t\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\"Code field mismatch: expected '%s', got '%s'\", options.ExpectedCode, errorResponse.Code),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Message contains expected substring (case-insensitive)\n\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" && errorResponse.Message != \"\" {\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(errorResponse.Message), strings.ToLower(options.MessageContains)) {\n\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tField:   \"Message\",\n\t\t\t\tPath:    \"Error.Message\",\n\t\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\"Message field does not contain expected substring '%s'\", options.MessageContains),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate custom fields (additional validation support)\n\tfor fieldName, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n\t\t// This is a placeholder for future custom field validation\n\t\t// For now, we just note that custom field validation would go here\n\t\t_ = fieldName\n\t\t_ = expectedValue\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// VALIDATION RESULT HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// String returns a human-readable representation of the validation result.\nfunc (r *ValidationResult) String() string {\n\tif r.Valid {\n\t\treturn \"Validation passed\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar parts []string\n\tparts = append(parts, \"Validation failed:\")\n\tfor _, err := range r.Errors {\n\t\tif err.Field != \"\" {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s (%s): %s\", err.Field, err.Path, err.Message))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\", err.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// GetErrorPaths returns the dot-notation paths of all fields that failed validation.\n//\n// This helper is useful for programmatically accessing validation errors\n// or for building custom error messages.\nfunc (r *ValidationResult) GetErrorPaths() []string {\n\tif r.Valid {\n\t\treturn []string{}\n\t}\n\n\tpaths := make([]string, 0, len(r.Errors))\n\tfor _, err := range r.Errors {\n\t\tif err.Path != \"\" {\n\t\t\tpaths = append(paths, err.Path)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn paths\n}\n\n// GetFieldErrors returns a map of field names to their error messages.\n//\n// This helper is useful for programmatically accessing per-field errors\n// or for building field-specific error reporting.\nfunc (r *ValidationResult) GetFieldErrors() map[string]string {\n\tif r.Valid {\n\t\treturn make(map[string]string)\n\t}\n\n\tfieldErrors := make(map[string]string)\n\tfor _, err := range r.Errors {\n\t\tif err.Field != \"\" {\n\t\t\tfieldErrors[err.Field] = err.Message\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn {\n\t// Valid indicates whether the validation passed\n\tValid bool\n\n\t// Errors is a list of validation errors (empty if Valid is true)\n\tErrors []ValidationError\n\n\t// ParsedError is the successfully parsed error structure (nil if parsing failed)\n\tParsedError *S3Error\n}\n\n// DefaultValidationOptions returns the default validation options.\n//\n// Defaults are:\n// - RequireCode: true\n// - RequireMessage: true\n// - MinMessageLength: 10\n// - ExpectedCode: \"\" (any code is valid)\n// - MessageContains: \"\" (no content check)\n// - CustomFields: empty map\n//\n// 10,\n\t\tMessageContains:  \"\",\n\t\tCustomFields:     make(map[string]string),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple validates error response structure with default options.\n//\n// This is a convenience function that uses default validation options.\n// It checks for:\n// - Valid XML structure\n// - Presence of Error root element\n// - Non-empty Code field (at least 10 characters)\n// - Non-empty Message field (at least 10 characters)\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - body: The raw response body bytes\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if validation passes, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   body := []byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?><Error><Code>TestCode</Code><Message>This is a test error message</Message></Error>`)\n//   result := ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, body)\nfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t *testing.T, body []byte) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\topts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n\tresult := ValidateErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(body, opts)\n\n\treturn result.Valid\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructure validates error response structure with custom options.\n//\n// This function provides full control over validation behavior through options.\n// It validates XML structure, required fields, field values, and content constraints.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - body: The raw response body bytes\n//   - options: Validation options to configure behavior\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if validation passes, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   opts := ErrorStructureValidationOptions{\n//       RequireCode:      true,\n//       RequireMessage:   true,\n//       ExpectedCode: ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tresult := ValidateErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(body, options)\n\n\t// If validation failed, report errors to test\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfor _, err := range result.Errors {\n\t\t\tif err.Field != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Field '%s' validation failed: %s\", err.Field, err.Message)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Validation failed: %s\", err.Message)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result.Valid\n}\n\n// AssertValidErrorResponseStructure asserts that the error response structure is valid.\n//\n// This is a convenience function for basic validation assertions.\n// It will fail the test if validation fails.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing fails, reporting detailed error information.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - body: The raw response body bytes\n//   - options: Validation options to {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tresult := ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, options)\n\tif !result {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed with options\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructureWithOptions performs the actual validation logic.\n//\n// This helper function contains the core validation logic used by all public\n// validation functions. It returns a detailed ValidationResult that can be\n// used for reporting or assertions.\nfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) is empty or missing\",\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate required Message field\n\tif options.RequireMessage {\n\t\ttrimmedMessage := strings.TrimSpace(errorResponse.Message)\n\t\tif Error Pattern Definitions (error_test_patterns.go):\n//   - Predefined error scenarios for common S3 errors (404, 403, 500, etc.)\n//   - Type-safe error structures (S3Error, ErrorScenarioConfig)\n//   - Error categorization by type (Auth, NotFound, Internal, etc.)\n//   - Mapping functions for error codes to patterns\n//\n// 2. HTTP Error Fixtures (http_error_fixtures.go):\n//   - Reusable HTTP error response fixtures\n//   - Configurable factory functions for common error codes\n//   - XML serialization for S3-compatible error responses\n//\n// 3. Test Infrastructure (error_test_infrastructure_test.go):\n//   - Test server and credential fixtures\n//   - Reusable test helpers for error validation\n//   - Setup/teardown utilities\n//\n// 4. Status Code Validation (error_status_validation.go):\n//   - Comprehensive status code validation helpers\n//   - Flexible assertion modes (boolean vs detailed error)\n//   - Multi-code validation support\n//\n// 5. Test Pattern Tables (error_test_patterns_base_test.go):\n//   - Table-driven test structures for consistency\n//   - Organized test cases by error category\n//   - Extensible patterns for custom scenarios\n//\n// # Quick Start\n//\n// Use a predefined pattern:\n//\n//\tpattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n//\tfmt.Printf(\"Expected status: %d\\n\", pattern.ExpectedStatus) // 404\n//\n// Get a pattern by error code:\n//\n//\tpattern := PatternForCode(\"NoSuchKey\")\n//\tfmt.Printf(\"Pattern: %s\\n\", pattern.Name)\n//\n// Get all patterns for a category:\n//\n//\tauthPatterns := PatternsForCategory(CategoryAuth)\n//\tfor _, p := range authPatterns {\n//\t    fmt.Printf(\"- %s\\n\", p.Name)\n//\t}\n//\n// Create a test fixture:\n//\n//\tfixture := NotFoundFixture(\"/api/blobs/missing-file.txt\")\n//\tresponse := fixture.ToXMLResponse()\n//\n// # Error Pattern Categories\n//\n//   - CommonErrorPatterns: 8 patterns covering the most common S3 error scenarios\n//   - AuthErrorPatterns: 6 patterns specifically for authentication failures\n//   - ClientErrorPatterns: 4 patterns for 4xx client errors\n//   - ServerErrorPatterns: 2 patterns for 5xx server errors\n//   - ErrorPatternByCategory: Map-based access to patterns by category\n//\n// # Type Safety\n//\n// All error structures use consistent, well-documented types:\n//   - S3Error: Core S3 error response structure\n//   - ErrorScenarioConfig: Test scenario configuration\n//   - ErrorResponseFixture: Complete response fixture with metadata\n//   - HTTPErrorFixture: HTTP-specific error fixture\n//   - ErrorResponseMetadata: Response metadata for logging/debugging\n//\n// # Testing Best Practices\n//\n// 1. Use predefined patterns when possible for consistency\n// 2. Extend patterns with custom configurations rather than creating from scratch\n// 3. Use category-based pattern access for organized testing\n// 4. Leverage table-driven tests for comprehensive coverage\n// 5. Document custom patterns with clear descriptions\n//\n// # Related Files\n//\n//   - error_pattern_usage_example.go: Comprehensive usage examples\n//   - error_test_patterns_test.go: Tests for the pattern infrastructure\n//   - error_structure_validation_test.go: Error structure validation tests\n//   - error_status_validation_test.go: Status code validation tests\n//   - error_response_verification_test.go: Response verification tests\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TEST PATTERNS - BASE TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n// This file provides foundational types and structures for error response testing.\n// It defines the common error structures that are used across both test and\n// non-test code, ensuring type consistency and reusability.\n//\n// Design Philosophy:\n// - Separation of concerns: base types in this file, test-specific helpers in test files\n// - Type consistency: shared types across test and non-test code\n// - Reusability: fixtures and patterns can be used anywhere\n// - Self-documenting: clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n//\n// Usage:\n//   - Test files import this package for base error structures\n//   - Non-test files (fixtures) use S3Error for type-safe error responses\n//   - Test patterns build on these base types\n// =============================================================================\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CORE ERROR TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// S3Error represents an S3 XML error response.\n//\n// This is the core type used across error testing, fixtures, and validation.\n// It matches the standard S3 error response format with Code and Message fields.\n//\n// Use this type when:\n//   - Parsing S3 XML error responses\n//   - Creating test fixtures that simulate S3 errors\n//   - Validating error response structure\n//   - Serializing errors to XML format\n//\n// Example (parsing):\n//\n//\tvar s3Err S3Error\n//\terr := xml.Unmarshal(responseBody, &s3Err)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    return err\n//\t}\n//\tfmt.Printf(\"Error code: %s, message: %s\\n\", s3Err.Code, s3Err.Message)\n//\n// Example (creating):\n//\n//\ts3Err := S3Error{\n//\t    Code: \"NoSuchKey\",\n//\t    Message: \"The specified key does not exist\",\n//\t}\n//\txmlBytes, _ := xml.Marshal(s3Err)\n//\n// Fields:\n//   - XMLName: XML element name (set to \"Error\" automatically)\n//   - Code: S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n//   - Message: Human-readable error message\ntype S3Error struct {\n\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Error\"`\n\tCode    string   `xml:\"Code\"`\n\tMessage string   `xml:\"Message\"`\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR SCENARIO TYPES (for non-test fixture usage)\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorScenarioConfig represents configuration for an error test scenario.\n//\n// This type is the primary configuration structure for defining expected error\n// behaviors in tests.
